notes: Smoke-only benchmark: proves the plugin provisions and exposes its skill entry points without driving any SDLC phase.
Provisioning uses an in-workspace marketplace, so nothing is installed at the global scope.
The negative verifier locks in the skills-over-slash-commands migration; a commands/ directory is a hard failure.
Launch from the repo root with: npm run eval:benchmarks
A working copy is retained only when a verifier or scenario fails, so investigators can inspect the exact failed state.

scenarios.title: Plugin install smoke test
scenarios.purpose: Confirm agentic-sdlc lands in a throwaway benchmark workspace and surfaces skill-based entry points, without kicking off any pipeline work.
scenarios.userInput: The agentic-sdlc plugin was just provisioned into this workspace through its local marketplace. Take a look at what got installed. Confirm the plugin is reachable via the in-workspace marketplace, that the repo-guides skill is available, and that the four sdlc-* control skills (sdlc-start, sdlc-autonomous, sdlc-status, sdlc-doctor) are present. Verify these run controls are wired up as skills rather than slash commands. Do not launch a full SDLC run of any kind -- this is discovery only.
successChecklist: The agent recognizes that the agentic-sdlc plugin has been installed and can point to how it was provisioned.
The agent confirms the repo-guides skill is present under the plugin.
The agent verifies the run controls are exposed as skills and not as slash commands.
The agent stops at discovery and never begins an end-to-end SDLC workflow.
